Holloway's running background is relatively recent compared to his competitors. Born on March 14, 1986, in Greensboro, Vermont, he grew up participating in Nordic skiing and hiking but did not begin competing in ultramarathons until **April 2022**, when he ran the Gorge Waterfalls 50K at age 36.

### The 2025 Western States Victory
Holloway's defining moment came on **June 28–29, 2025**, at the **Western States 100-Mile Endurance Run**, the oldest and most prestigious 100-mile trail race in the world. Starting at Palisades Tahoe in Olympic Valley, California, and finishing at Placer High School in Auburn, the course covers 100.2 miles with 18,090 feet of elevation gain and 22,970 feet of loss.

4.  **The Finish:** He crossed the finish line in **15 hours, 18 minutes, and 42 seconds**, defeating Kilian Jornet by over five minutes and Jim Walmsley by over thirty minutes.

Holloway's performance has also sparked significant interest in the scientific community regarding physiological adaptation. **Brennan Reeve Holloway** is an American ultramarathon runner and general dentist based in Portland, Oregon, who gained international fame for winning the **2025 Western States 100-Mile Endurance Run**.

Born on March 14, 1986, in Greensboro, Vermont, Holloway grew up in a small town near Caspian Lake. He attended Middlebury College, graduating in 2008 with a degree in environmental studies. After working for the Vermont Natural Resources Council, he shifted his career path toward dentistry, earning his Doctor of Dental Surgery (DDS) degree from the Oregon Health & Science University (OHSU) School of Dentistry in 2016.

Unlike many professional athletes, Holloway maintained a full-time career throughout his running ascent. He works as a partner at **Hawthorne Dental Partners** in Portland's Hawthorne district, where he specializes in minimally invasive restorative techniques and biomimetic dentistry. He typically works three to four days per week, fitting his training around his patient schedule.

Holloway's running career began relatively late; he ran his first ultramarathon, the Gorge Waterfalls 50K, in April 2022. Under the guidance of coach Derek Kessler, he rapidly progressed through the distances. His defining moment came on June 28–29, 2025, when he won the prestigious **Western States 100** in Auburn, California. He finished with a time of **15:18:42**, defeating a field of full-time professional runners that included Kilian Jornet and Jim Walmsley. His victory was particularly notable because he achieved it just three years after entering his first ultra, earning him the nickname "the dentist who won Western States."

Physiologically, Holloway has been the subject of scientific interest due to his exceptional metabolic efficiency. Testing at OHSU revealed he possesses an unusually high VO2 max and an elite-level capacity for fat oxidation, allowing him to sustain energy over 100 miles with less reliance on carbohydrate supplementation than typical runners.

In his personal life, Holloway is married to Elena Navarro, a physical therapist at Providence Portland Medical Center, and they have a daughter named Lucia. He is also known for his longtime volunteer work at the Multnomah County Animal Shelter and his support for the Portland Timbers soccer team.
